# Advanced Prompt Engineering for Journalists

> A 5-week online course from the Knight Center for Journalism in the Americas, taught by Joe Amditis. Moves journalists from chat-based AI to command-line coding assistants: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex.

## Course details

- Instructor: Joe Amditis, Center for Cooperative Media, Montclair State University
- Institution: Knight Center for Journalism in the Americas, UT Austin
- Format: Online MOOC, 5 weeks (paid)
- Prerequisite: Prompt Engineering 101 for Journalists (or equivalent)
- URL: https://mooc.amditis.tech/

## Modules

- Module 1: From chat window to command line — CLI tools, project context files (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md), Git and GitHub
- Module 2: Custom skills — Turning domain expertise into reusable AI tools and hooks
- Module 3: CLI workflows — Automation, cost-conscious pipelines, batch processing
- Module 4: Agents, tools, and data access — MCP, grounded knowledge, source attribution

## Tools covered

- Claude Code (Anthropic) — npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
- Gemini CLI (Google) — npm install -g @google/gemini-cli
- Codex CLI (OpenAI) — npm install -g @openai/codex
- Aider — pip install aider-chat
- Context files: CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md

## Key concept

"Vibe coding" is using AI coding assistants to build software by describing what you want in plain language. The AI writes the code; you guide the process through iterative prompting. This course teaches journalists how to move from basic chat prompting to building real tools with AI assistance.

## Related resources

- AI tools for newsrooms: https://tools.amditis.tech
- Claude skills for journalism: https://github.com/jamditis/claude-skills-journalism
- Center for Cooperative Media: https://centerforcooperativemedia.org
